nodes.label: פילון האלכסנדרוני
nodes.bio: Hellenistic Jewish philosopher. Pioneered allegorical interpretation of Scripture. Bridged Jewish and Greek philosophical traditions.

nodes.label: רבי עקיבא
nodes.bio: One of the greatest Tannaim. Developed systematic hermeneutics of Torah. Martyred during Bar Kokhba Revolt.

nodes.label: רבי מאיר
nodes.bio: Great Tanna known for brilliant interpretations. Famous for creative hermeneutical methods. Student of Rabbi Akiva.

nodes.label: רבה בר בר חנה
nodes.bio: Master of mystical and allegorical stories. Teachings on exile and redemption through imaginative narratives.

nodes.label: רבנו גרשום
nodes.bio: Light of the Diaspora. Founder of Northern French Ashkenazi school. Established halakhic standards for European Jewry.

nodes.label: רמב"ם
nodes.bio: Maimonides. Greatest medieval Jewish philosopher. Synthesized Aristotelian philosophy with Torah. Codified halakha systematically.

nodes.label: רמב"ן
nodes.bio: Nahmanides. Master of Kabbalah and biblical exegesis. Defender of tradition against Maimonidean rationalism.

nodes.label: הרמח"ל
nodes.bio: Ramchal. Mystical philosopher. Author of Mesillat Yesharim. Mystical systematizer of Jewish thought.

nodes.label: רבי ישראל בן אליעזר
nodes.bio: Baal Shem Tov. Founder of Hasidic movement. Revolutionized Jewish spirituality and practice.

nodes.label: הרב אברהם יצחק קוק
nodes.bio: Chief Rabbi of Palestine. Philosopher and Kabbalist. Visionary thinker on Zionism and Judaism.
